2604.09158.title: Structuring versus Problematizing: How LLM-based Agents Scaffold Learning in Diagnostic Reasoning
2604.09158.paper_summary: Supporting students in developing diagnostic reasoning is a key challenge across educational domains. Novices often face cognitive biases such as premature closure and over-reliance on heuristics, and they struggle to transfer diagnostic strategies to new cases. Scenario-based learning (SBL) enhanced by Learning Analytics (LA) and large language models (LLM) offers a promising approach by combining realistic case experiences with personalized scaffolding. Yet, how different scaffolding approaches shape reasoning processes remains insufficiently explored. This study introduces PharmaSim Switch, an SBL environment for pharmacy technician training, extended with an LA- and LLM-powered pharmacist agent that implements pedagogical conversations rooted in two theory-driven scaffolding approaches: \emph{structuring} and \emph{problematizing}, as well as a student learning trajectory. In a between-groups experiment, 63 vocational students completed a learning scenario, a near-transfer scenario, and a far-transfer scenario under one of the two scaffolding conditions. Results indicate that both scaffolding approaches were effective in supporting the use of diagnostic strategies. Performance outcomes were primarily influenced by scenario complexity rather than students' prior knowledge or the scaffolding approach used. The structuring approach was associated with more accurate Active and Interactive participation, whereas problematizing elicited more Constructive engagement. These findings underscore the value of combining scaffolding approaches when designing LA- and LLM-based systems to effectively foster diagnostic reasoning.
2604.09158.paper_summary_zh: 支持學生發展診斷推理能力是各教育領域的一項關鍵挑戰。<lb/>新手常常面臨認知偏見，例如過早結論和過度依賴啟發式，並且他們在將診斷策略轉移到新案例時遇到困難。<lb/>基於情境的學習（SBL）結合學習分析（LA）和大型語言模型（LLM）提供了一種有前景的方法，通過將現實案例經驗與個性化支架相結合。<lb/>然而，不同的支架方法如何塑造推理過程仍然缺乏充分的探索。<lb/>本研究介紹了PharmaSim Switch，一個用於藥劑技術員培訓的SBL環境，並擴展了由LA和LLM驅動的藥劑師代理，該代理實施基於兩種理論驅動的支架方法的教學對話：\emph{結構化}和\emph{問題化}，以及學生學習軌跡。<lb/>在一項組間實驗中，63名職業學生在兩種支架條件下完成了一個學習情境、一個近轉移情境和一個遠轉移情境。<lb/>結果表明，這兩種支架方法在支持使用診斷策略方面都是有效的。<lb/>表現結果主要受到情境複雜性的影響，而不是學生的先前知識或使用的支架方法。<lb/>結構化方法與更準確的主動和互動參與相關，而問題化則引發了更多的建設性參與。<lb/>這些發現強調了在設計基於LA和LLM的系統時結合支架方法的價值，以有效促進診斷推理。
2604.09158.author: Fatma Betül Güreş et.al.
2604.09158.authors: Fatma Betül Güreş, Tanya Nazaretsky, Seyed Parsa Neshaei, Tanja Käser
